channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Issues Patches for SharePoint Zero-Day and 168 Other New Vulnerabilities
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released updates to address a record 169 security flaws across its product portfolio, including one vulnerability that has been actively exploited in the wild. Of these 169 vulner...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4-Cyber with Expanded Access for Security Teams
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday unveiled GPT-5.4-Cyber, a variant of its latest flagship model, GPT‑5.4, that's specifically optimized for defensive cybersecurity use cases, days after rival Anthropic unveiled its ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Bets $10 Billion to Boost Japan's AI, Cybersecurity
item.description: The deal aims to accelerate AI adoption, train workers, and develop cybersecurity partnerships — the latest move by a hyperscaler to compete for sovereign AI and data centers....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft adds Windows protections for malicious Remote Desktop files
item.description: Microsoft has introduced new Windows protections to defend against phishing attacks that abuse Remote Desktop connection (.rdp) files, adding warnings and disabling risky shared resources by default. ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Crypto-exchange Kraken extorted by hackers after insider breach
item.description: The Kraken cryptocurrency exchange announced that a cybercrime group is trying to extort the company by threatening to release videos showing internal systems that host client data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly di...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
item.description: Elevation-of-privilege bugs accounted for more than half of the 165 vulnerabilities patched, with two zero-days in that mix....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Over 100 Chrome Web Store extensions steal user accounts, data
item.description: More than 100 malicious extensions in the official Chrome Web Store are attempting to steal Google OAuth2 Bearer tokens, deploy backdoors, and carry out ad fraud. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: EDR-Killer Ecosystem Expansion Requires Stronger BYOVD Defenses
item.description: Stopping EDR killers, which employ bring-your-own-vulnerable-driver (BYOVD) attack techniques, is difficult, but not impossible....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5082200 extended security update
item.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5082200 extended security update to fix the April 2026 Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities, including 2 zero-days. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: McGraw-Hill confirms data breach following extortion threat
item.description: Education company McGraw-Hill has confirmed in a statement to BleepingComputer that hackers exploited a Salesforce misconfiguration and accessed its internal data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Windows 11 cumulative updates KB5083769 & KB5082052 released
item.description: Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5083769 and KB5082052 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft April 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes 167 flaws, 2 zero-days
item.description: Today is Microsoft's April 2026 Patch Tuesday with security updates for 167 flaws, including 2 zero-day vulnerabilities. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake Ledger Live app on Apple’s App Store stole $9.5M in crypto
item.description: A malicious Ledger Live app for macOS available from Apple's App Store has drained approximately $9.5 million in cryptocurrency from 50 victims in just a few days this month. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: War Game Exercise Demonstrates How Social Media Manipulation Works
item.description: In an educational game called "Capture the Narrative," students created bots to sway a fictional election, simulating influence in real-world political scenarios....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New PHP Composer Flaws Enable Arbitrary Command Execution — Patches Released
item.description: Two high-severity security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in Composer, a package manager for PHP, that, if successfully exploited, could result in arbitrary command execution. The vulnerabilities...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft rolls out fast-track to reinstate Windows hardware dev accounts
item.description: Microsoft has rolled out a fast-track process to help developers regain access to accounts recently suspended from its Windows Hardware Program, following widespread complaints that they were locked o...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google Adds Rust-Based DNS Parser into Pixel 10 Modem to Enhance Security
item.description: Google has announced the integration of a Rust-based Domain Name System (DNS) parser into the modem firmware as part of its ongoing efforts to beef up the security of Pixel devices and push memory-saf...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AI-Driven Pushpaganda Scam Exploits Google Discover to Spread Scareware and Ad Fraud
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have unmasked a novel ad fraud scheme that has been found to leverage search engine poisoning (SEO) techniques and artificial intelligence (AI)-generated content to push dece...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
item.description: Stolen credentials remain a top breach vector, often leading to unchecked privilege escalation. Specops explains how identity-first Zero Trust limits access, enforces device trust, and blocks lateral ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Mirax Android RAT Turns Devices into SOCKS5 Proxies, Reaching 220,000 via Meta Ads
item.description: A nascent Android remote access trojan called Mirax has been observed actively targeting Spanish-speaking countries, with campaigns reaching more than 220,000 accounts on Facebook, Instagram, Messenge...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Analysis of 216M Security Findings Shows a 4x Increase In Critical Risk (2026 Report)
item.description: OX Security recently analyzed 216 million security findings across 250 organizations over a 90-day period. The primary takeaway: while raw alert volume grew by 52% year-over-year, prioritized critical...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 108 Malicious Chrome Extensions Steal Google and Telegram Data, Affecting 20,000 Users
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new campaign in which a cluster of 108 Google Chrome extensions has been found to communicate with the same command-and-control (C2) infrastructure with the...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ShowDoc RCE Flaw CVE-2025-0520 Actively Exploited on Unpatched Servers
item.description: A critical security vulnerability impacting ShowDoc, a document management and collaboration service popular in China, has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability in question is ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA Adds 6 Known Exploited Flaws in Fortinet, Microsoft, and Adobe Software
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added half a dozen security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitatio...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: European Gym giant Basic-Fit data breach affects 1 million members
item.description: Dutch fitness giant Basic-Fit announced that hackers breached its systems and gained access to information belonging to a million of its customers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Why Orgs Need to Test Networks to Withstand DDoS Attacks During Peak Loads
item.description: Security teams can't test distributed denial-of-service defenses in a vacuum. They need to test during periods of high demand, such as tax filing deadlines....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CSA: CISOs Should Prepare for Post-Mythos Exploit Storm
item.description: In a new report from the Cloud Security Alliance (CSA), experts warn of an "AI vulnerability storm" triggered by the introduction of Anthropic's Claude Mythos....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Zero-Day That Lingered for Months
item.description: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Stolen Rockstar Games analytics data leaked by extortion gang
item.description: Rockstar Games has suffered a data breach linked to a recent security incident at Anodot, with the ShinyHunters extortion gang now leaking the stolen data on its data leak site. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
